title: Postman の 403 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately explains the HTTP 403 Forbidden error and its common causes in the context of API testing with Postman.
The suggested solutions, such as checking API key scopes, IP whitelisting, and permission levels, are standard and effective troubleshooting steps.
Postman-specific features like Environment Variables and Pre-request Scripts are correctly introduced as tools to manage and debug API requests.
Most factual claims are supported by multiple, credible, and up-to-date sources, including official documentation from Postman, API providers, and web standards.

sources.title: 403 Forbidden - HTTP - MDN Web Docs
sources.claim: HTTP 403 Forbidden is a client error response status code indicating that the server understood the request but refuses to authorize it, often due to insufficient permissions, even if authentication was successful.

sources.title: HTTP 403 - Wikipedia
sources.claim: HTTP 403, or the error message Forbidden, is an HTTP status code indicating that a page exists but the specific accessor does not have permission to view it, and access is denied.

sources.title: 403 Forbiddenとは？意味・原因と解決方法を解説 - ウィルゲート
sources.claim: 403 Forbidden is an HTTP status code displayed when accessing a website, meaning 'access forbidden', where the server recognized the request but denied viewing due to insufficient access rights, which can be caused by intentionally restricting access from specific IP addresses or misconfigured access permissions.

sources.title: 403エラーとは？突然403 forbiddenが発生した時の対処法
sources.claim: 403 Forbidden error can be caused by access permission setting errors or incorrect specific IP address access restriction settings.

sources.title: 突然403エラー（403 Forbidden）に！原因と対処法をチェック | 株式会社PLAN-B
sources.claim: 403 Forbidden is an HTTP status code that rejects the execution of a request, primarily indicating that access (viewing) to the attempted website is denied due to lack of appropriate permissions, such as permission setting errors or unauthorized users accessing administrator-only screens.

sources.title: API キー権限の割り当て - OpenAI Help Center
sources.claim: API keys can have permission levels (scopes) such as 'All', 'Restricted', and 'Read Only', allowing specific access to resources/endpoints, for example, an API key with only read-only scope cannot perform write operations.

sources.title: APIキーの管理 - ドキュメント - SendGrid
sources.claim: API keys have different access levels such as 'No Access', 'Read Access' (GET only), and 'Full Access' (all HTTP verbs), and an API key cannot be created with stronger permissions than its creator.

sources.title: Explain the use of environment variables in Postman requests. - GeeksforGeeks
sources.claim: Postman environment variables act as placeholders for dynamic values that can be reused across multiple requests, allowing for efficient API testing and development by managing dynamic values easily.

sources.title: A Complete Guide: How to Use Environment Variables in Postman - EchoAPI
sources.claim: Postman environment variables are associated with a specific environment and represent configuration values or data used during API development and testing, enabling dynamic setting of values for request URLs, headers, and body data.

sources.title: Managing Postman environments with the Postman API - Learning Lab
sources.claim: A Postman environment is a set of variables that can be reused in requests and shared with a team, allowing for efficient work and dynamic workflows by referencing them in authentication, parameters, and scripts.

sources.title: Postmanで403 Forbiddenエラーを修正する方法 - Apidog
sources.claim: When a 403 Forbidden error occurs in Postman, it means the server recognizes the request but refuses to authorize it, often due to a lack of necessary permissions or an invalid/expired API key.

sources.title: How to create Postman Environment Variables? | by Saloni Jain - Medium
sources.claim: Postman Environment Variables are placeholders for dynamic data within Postman APIs and development tools, used to store and manage variables and their values for flexible and powerful API requests.

sources.title: APIキーについて - microCMSドキュメント
sources.claim: API keys are authentication keys used for authentication and access control when using APIs, determining the access permissions (e.g., read, write) that an API user has, and can prevent external write requests depending on their settings.

sources.title: 2026最新：Postmanの環境変数の使い方を完全解説！ - Apidog
sources.claim: Postman environment variables are variables associated with a Postman environment, representing configuration values or data used during API development and testing, enabling dynamic values for request URLs, headers, and body data for easy switching and reuse.

sources.title: Debug API requests in Postman
sources.claim: The Postman Console displays network information, request and response headers and body for each request, along with any console output messages from scripts.

sources.title: How to see request with headers and body data with variables substituted - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: The Postman Console, accessible from the bottom left, allows users to inspect requests, including headers and body data, after sending them.

sources.title: API response structure in Postman
sources.claim: An API response in Postman consists of the response body, headers, cookies, and the HTTP status code, and users can view these details.

sources.title: Postmanのテスト実行オプションとそれぞれの実行場所 (ローカル or クラウド)と注意点について
sources.claim: When API requests are sent from Postman Cloud, the source IP will be one of Postman Cloud's IP ranges. Since Postman Cloud is built on AWS and does not provide specific fixed IP ranges, allowing access may require whitelisting broad AWS IP address ranges.

sources.title: What IP ranges does Postman use to send requests? – Postman
sources.claim: When sending requests from the Postman web app with a cloud agent, requests are sent from the Postman cloud, which does not support static IPs and runs on AWS data centers, requiring users to refer to AWS IP address ranges for whitelisting.

sources.title: スコープ付き API キー - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: Scoped API keys are supported to allow for more granular permissions, giving better control over API access.

sources.title: API トークンの権限 | Bitbucket Cloud | アトラシアン サポート - Atlassian Support
sources.claim: API tokens can be created with restricted permissions (scopes) such as read, write, admin, and delete, which define the high-level operations that can be performed against API endpoints.

sources.title: APIキーについて - fincode Docs
sources.claim: API keys have different types, such as public keys and secret keys, with varying levels of permitted operations; for example, public keys allow limited operations, while secret keys permit all operations allowed to the merchant.

sources.title: Pre-request scripts | Invicti Platform
sources.claim: Pre-request scripts in Postman allow for manipulation of requests before sending them to the target, enabling logic to be implemented for tasks such as adding headers or performing pre-validation.

sources.title: 403（Forbidden）エラーとは？原因や種類・突然発生したときの解決方法を解説 - AIsmiley
sources.claim: 403 Forbidden is an HTTP status code indicating that the web server understood the request but access to the resource is forbidden due to lack of access rights.

sources.title: 403 Forbiddenエラーとは？5つの原因と対処法を解説 | デジ研
sources.claim: A 403 Forbidden error is an HTTP status code indicating that a page is forbidden to be viewed, meaning you do not have permission to access that page.

sources.title: Is it possible to know the IP address of monitors in Postman? · Issue #9807 - GitHub
sources.claim: Postman Monitors run on Amazon's AWS infrastructure, and specific IP addresses for whitelisting are not provided.

sources.title: API keys generation and IP Address whitelisting | Postman v2 Guide
sources.claim: IP addresses of each agency will be whitelisted for Postman API integration.

sources.title: 403 Forbiddenの原因と解決方法について - キーワードファインダー
sources.claim: HTTP 403, or the error message Forbidden, is an HTTP status code indicating that a page exists but specific access is denied due to lack of permission, which can be caused by site administrator errors like incorrect permissions or intentional access restrictions using .htaccess.

sources.title: Postman Pre-request Scripts - Brian Ponath
sources.claim: Postman's Pre-request Script executes before the actual request, allowing for manipulation of requests, such as dynamically setting authentication tokens stored in environment variables.

sources.title: Pre-Request Script scopes in Postman - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Postman's pre-request scripts can access and manipulate variables across different scopes (global, collection, environment) using the `pm` API.

sources.title: How to ガイド - API キー認証 - Workato docs
sources.claim: Permissions assigned to an API key can follow the permissions of the user who generated it or be configured per key, determining access rights for API usage.

sources.title: Controlling variable scope to request level - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: Postman's pre-request scripts can be used to generate and assign values to variables, which can then be used in the request body, allowing for pre-validation or dynamic data generation.

sources.title: Scheduled Postman Runs and IP whitelisting - Help Hub
sources.claim: Postman scheduled runs use too many dynamic IP addresses for practical whitelisting, with a static IP option available only for Postman Professional plans.

sources.title: APIアクセススコープを作成する
sources.claim: Scopes represent high-level operations that can be performed against API endpoints, with authorization servers determining which scopes to grant or deny permissions for.

sources.title: Postman テストスクリプトリファレンス #API - Qiita
sources.claim: Postman's pre-request scripts (and other scripts) can use the `pm` API to access and manipulate variables across various scopes, and can dynamically change the execution order for collection workflows.

sources.title: Google API の OAuth 2.0 スコープ
sources.claim: OAuth 2.0 scopes define the level of access an application requests to Google APIs, determining what information it can view, edit, configure, or delete.

unsupported_claims: Postman Interceptor での送信元IP確認
improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Clarify or update the section on 'Postman Interceptor での送信元IP確認' as current official documentation doesn't prominently feature 'Interceptor' for directly confirming source IPs for 403 troubleshooting. It might be better to refer to checking network settings or Postman documentation on cloud agent IPs.
Optional improvement: Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.
